title: PV Sindhu Conquers Japan Open, Ends Two-Year Title Drought
indian_angle: PV Sindhu's victory marks her first title in two years and makes her the first Indian to win the Japan Open.
summary: Indian badminton star PV Sindhu defeated Akane Yamaguchi in straight games to claim the Japan Open title, ending a significant title drought and silencing doubters.

title: Reengineered to Win: Inside Sindhu’s Radical, Data-Driven Comeback
indian_angle: This article details the scientific approach and hard work by sports scientist Wayne Lombard that propelled PV Sindhu back to her top form.
summary: Sports scientist Wayne Lombard reveals the meticulous, data-driven strategy behind PV Sindhu's resurgence, which led to her recent Japan Open triumph after two challenging years.

title: Major Venue Revamp Underway for World Badminton Championships in Delhi
indian_angle: India is preparing to host the World Championships in Delhi with significant venue upgrades to prevent past embarrassments like animal intrusions.
summary: Following issues at the India Open, a major revamp is underway for the Delhi venue set to host the World Badminton Championships, addressing concerns like bird droppings and stray monkeys.

title: Indian Shuttlers Should Learn to Think on Their Feet, Win Without a Coach
indian_angle: This opinion piece advocates for Indian badminton players to develop independent thinking and strategy on court, crucial for international success.
summary: An analysis suggests Indian shuttlers need to cultivate more self-reliance and tactical thinking during matches, enabling them to win even without constant coaching guidance.

title: Badminton's 3x15 Scoring to Debut in All Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: A new 3x15 scoring format will be implemented across all domestic badminton tournaments in India, starting in Ernakulam.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced a new 3x15 scoring system for all domestic tournaments, aiming to debut the format at an event in Ernakulam.
